ddls:
CREATE TABLE "campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each campaign */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the campaign */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "budget" TEXT /* Budget allocated for the campaign */,
  "expected_revenue" TEXT /* Expected revenue from the campaign */,
  "actual_revenue" TEXT /* Actual revenue generated by the campaign */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the campaign (e.g., planned, active, completed) */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the campaign manager responsible for the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaigns_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segment */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the segment */,
  "target_age_range" TEXT /* Age range of the target customers */,
  "target_location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the target customers */,
  "target_income_range" TEXT /* Income range of the target customers */,
  "target_gender" TEXT /* Gender of the target customers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_channels" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each channel */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the channel */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the channel */,
  "reach" INTEGER /* Estimated number of people reached by the channel */,
  "cost_per_reach" TEXT /* Cost per person reached by the channel */,
  "engagement_rate" TEXT /* Average engagement rate (e.g., click-through rate, open rate) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each result */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the campaign */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment */,
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the channel */,
  "positives" INTEGER /* Number of positive responses */,
  "negatives" INTEGER /* Number of negative responses */,
  "success_rate" REAL /* Success rate of the campaign */,
  "z_ratio" REAL /* Z-ratio of the campaign */,
  "lift" REAL /* Lift of the campaign */,
  "total_reach" INTEGER /* Total number of people reached by the campaign */,
  "total_engagement" INTEGER /* Total number of engagements (e.g., clicks, opens) */,
  "conversion_rate" REAL /* Conversion rate of the campaign */,
  "revenue_generated" TEXT /* Revenue generated from the campaign */,
  "cost" TEXT /* Total cost of the campaign */,
  "roi" REAL /* Return on investment (ROI) of the campaign */,
  "feedback_score" REAL /* Average feedback score from customers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_results_channel_id FOREIGN KEY ("channel_id") REFERENCES marketing_channels ("channel_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_results_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES customer_segments ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_results_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the campaign */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment */,
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the channel */,
  "predicted_response" TEXT /* Predicted response of the customer */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score of the prediction */,
  "prediction_date" TEXT /* Date when the prediction was made */,
  "model_version" TEXT /* Version of the machine learning model used for the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_channel_id FOREIGN KEY ("channel_id") REFERENCES marketing_channels ("channel_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES customer_segments ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., campaign manager, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the user account is active */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the user has admin privileges */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Actual revenue generated by the campaign

Age range of the target customers
